name: Al-Furqaan Foundation
mission: To inform and educate all the residents of the United States the truth about Islam and its core principals and to counter the negative media information...
theoryOfChange: Al-Furqaan Foundation operates with a clear mission to deliver the message of The Quran to every individual in America, aiming to fulfill what they identify as the '5 rights of The Quran' upon Muslims: to Believe It, Recite It, Understand It, Act Upon It, and Convey It. This mission serves as their core strategic framework, guiding their various initiatives. Their work is structured across three primary verticals: Dawah Projects, Educational Projects, and Service Projects, each designed to make the Quran's message accessible. Examples of these projects include The Furqaan Project (Quran distribution), The Clear Islam (street dawah), Islam in Prison, Dawah in Spanish, Dawah in Braille, The New Muslim Foundation, and Furqaan Academy (educational institutions). The organization's vision for the future includes expanding dawah outreach globally and strengthening educational institutions to nurture confident, faithful Muslim leaders, and ensuring new Muslims find a supportive environment.
scoreSummary: AL-FURQAAN FOUNDATION shows good alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Al-Furqaan Foundation explicitly states, "Donate your zakat today." Their donation pages also include an option to select "Zakat/Sadaqa" when choosing the number of Quran boxes to sponsor and as a general donation designation. Masjid Furqaan, a division of Al-Furqaan Foundation, also has a dedicated "Zakat Distribution" section on its website. (Source: https://furqaan.org/giving/)

While it holds 25.1 months of working capital, the institutional nature of its book distribution leads to a high calculated cost per beneficiary [4][5].

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in the foundation's <cite id="4">audited financial statements</cite> and its long history, having been <cite id="1">founded in 2003</cite>. However, credibility is tempered by a <cite id="3">board size of only 1 member</cite> listed in recent filings, which is below standard governance recommendations.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should expand its board to include at least five independent members and publish updated annual impact reports.

impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Tracking distribution metrics ensures that donor funds are successfully converted into tangible educational resources for the public.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="3">89.3% of its total expenses</cite> toward its educational and distribution programs, significantly exceeding industry benchmarks for lean operations.
The foundation spends only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions, ensuring that donor funds are not lost to high marketing or solicitation costs.
The foundation has successfully distributed <cite id="4">855,697 books</cite> as part of its mission to educate the public on Islamic principles.

strengths_deep_dive: Al-Furqaan Foundation's operational strength lies in its ability to maintain a massive distribution network with minimal overhead. By focusing on <cite id="5">products offered through its website</cite> and direct outreach, it has scaled its impact to include <cite id="5">Dawah in Braille and prison chaplaincy support</cite>. This specialized focus allows the organization to serve niche populations that are often overlooked by larger humanitarian agencies. Furthermore, the foundation's <cite id="3">total assets of $9,389,287</cite> reflect a well-capitalized institution capable of sustaining its long-term vision of nationwide religious literacy.
rich_narrative.summary: founded in 2003, Al-Furqaan Foundation is a Chicago-based Islamic educational organization dedicated to distributing religious literature and countering misconceptions about Islam across the United States. The foundation operates several specialized initiatives, including the <cite id="5">Furqaan Project and Furqaan Dawah Center</cite>, which focus on the dissemination of the Quran and educational materials. Financially, the organization demonstrates a high degree of resource allocation toward its core mission, reporting a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 89.3%</cite> based on its most recent comprehensive financial filings. With <cite id="3">total revenue of $3,249,437</cite>, the foundation maintains a lean administrative structure, evidenced by its <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.01 per $1 raised</cite>. Beyond general distribution, the organization has expanded its reach through programs like <cite id="5">Islam in Prison and Dawah in Braille</cite>, ensuring that religious resources are accessible to underserved and marginalized populations. The foundation's long-term stability is supported by significant liquidity, maintaining <cite id="1">25.1 months of working capital</cite> in working capital, which provides a substantial buffer for its ongoing educational projects.
